page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6e-c12g1.3xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 12 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 physical cores operating at 2.5 GHz with single-thread-per-core execution. The system is equipped with 92.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.67 GB per core. Acceleration is delivered via a single integrated NVIDIA Volta V100 GPU with 32 GB of VRAM. This instance does not include local storage and relies on external storage options, while network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear division between CPU and GPU capabilities. Single-core CPU performance is relatively weak, scoring in the bottom 10% to 25% in stress-ng and Geekbench tests. Multi-core performance is average, placing in the middle 50% across Geekbench and PassMark suites. However, the GPU-accelerated architecture excels in machine learning workloads. LLM inference benchmarks for a medium 7B model show top-tier performance, reaching the top 10% for both prompt processing at 1350.18 tokens per second and text generation at 114.19 tokens per second.

From a resource density perspective, the instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that can offload intensive computations to the dedicated 32 GB V100 GPU, offsetting the lower single-core CPU performance. It is highly suited for deep learning, LLM inference on small to medium models, and GPU-accelerated parallel processing tasks. It is less optimal for single-threaded CPU-bound applications or workloads requiring high-speed local storage.

It is configured with 12 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz, 92.0 GB of RAM, and a single NVIDIA Volta V100 GPU with 32 GB of VRAM. The instance does not feature local storage and provides a 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show weak single-core CPU performance but average multi-core capabilities. Conversely, the hardware excels in machine learning tasks, delivering top-tier LLM inference speeds for medium-sized models. This profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for GPU-dependent workloads, such as deep learning and model inference, while being less suited for CPU-bound or local storage-heavy applications.
